intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

body.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

draft.title: recipe app for people who hate cooking
draft.summary: Drafting your prototype from the swipes…
draft.html:
recipe app for people who hate cooking

Building your first draft…

data.intent: recipe app for people who hate cooking

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer warm, low-contrast interfaces that feel intimate rather than bright and clinical
facade.label: Dark & cozy
facade.acceptImplies: Explore dark mode, warm color palettes, soft shadows for a comforting feel
facade.rejectImplies: Test bright, high-contrast, clinical aesthetics instead

evidence.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer warm, low-contrast interfaces that feel intimate rather than bright and clinical
evidence.implication: Explore dark mode, warm color palettes, soft shadows for a comforting feel

facade.hypothesis: Users expect immediate action through gesture rather than clicking buttons, reducing perceived friction in the recipe app workflow
facade.label: Swipe to start cooking
facade.acceptImplies: Fluid, motion-forward design where users feel they're 'in control' through physical gestures; transitions should feel responsive and natural
facade.rejectImplies: Users prefer explicit, labeled buttons and clear step-by-step navigation; gestures may feel ambiguous or require learning

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking prefer stripped-down, friction-free design that gets them to recipes quickly without decorative elements or culinary storytelling
facade.axisTargeted: organic vs digital; calm vs energetic
facade.label: Minimal, no-fuss interface
facade.acceptImplies: The app should prioritize speed and clarity over visual warmth or personality. Design should feel utilitarian.
facade.rejectImplies: Users want the app to feel more inviting, encouraging, or human—design should include warmth, personality, or visual interest to make cooking feel less intimidating.

facade.hypothesis: People who hate cooking want minimal narrative—just what to buy, no story or steps visible upfront
facade.axisTargeted: Dense data (ingredient checklist) vs narrative (recipe storytelling)
facade.label: Just the ingredients list
facade.acceptImplies: Users prefer scannable, action-oriented info design; they want to see commitments (ingredients) before diving into method
facade.rejectImplies: Users want context and narrative embedded early; they need the 'why' and 'how' story to feel motivated, not just a shopping list

facade.hypothesis: A voice that mocks the cooking process itself—winking at user frustration rather than pretending to be a cheerful cooking companion—will feel more aligned with someone who hates cooking
facade.label: Sarcastic & sassy
facade.acceptImplies: User resonates with irreverent, comedic tone; app personality should lean into self-aware humor about cooking struggles
facade.rejectImplies: User prefers practical, no-nonsense tone; humor should be minimal or self-effacing rather than sarcastic; personality should feel more like a helpful tool than a witty friend

facade.hypothesis: For recipe-haters, massive single-recipe full-screen cards with minimal steps reduce cognitive load better than dense lists or text-heavy layouts
facade.axisTargeted: card-based vs list-based; spacious vs dense
facade.label: Giant recipe cards
facade.acceptImplies: User prefers breathing room and one-thing-at-a-time presentation—explore large touch targets, full-bleed imagery, progressive disclosure of ingredients/steps
facade.rejectImplies: User may prefer scannable overviews, compact reference layouts, or ability to see multiple recipes at once—explore grids, tabs, or sidebar navigation instead

axes.label: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
Whether user prefers step-by-step guided workflows vs. ingredient-first browsing and flexible navigation
Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. irreverent, humorous tone that validates cooking aversion
Whether user wants calm, meditative cooking experience vs. quick-win, dopamine-hit satisfaction from easy wins
Whether user wants minimal information (just do this) vs. detailed explanations (why this works, what you're learning)
axes.poleA: Just tell me or explain why

axes.label: Whether user prefers instant gratification with no waiting vs. anticipation-building with timers and progress feedback

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants visual simplicity and minimal ingredient lists vs. rich food photography and detailed plating inspiration
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Iris

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers step-by-step guided workflows vs. ingredient-first browsing and flexible navigation
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Prism

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user responds to encouraging, motivational tone vs. irreverent, humorous tone that validates cooking aversion
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Lumen

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants calm, meditative cooking experience vs. quick-win, dopamine-hit satisfaction from easy wins
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Aura

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user wants minimal information (just do this) vs. detailed explanations (why this works, what you're learning)
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Facet

scout_assignments.probe_axis: Whether user prefers instant gratification with no waiting vs. anticipation-building with timers and progress feedback
scout_assignments.reason: Cold start: first question for Echo

facade.hypothesis: Users who hate cooking may prefer clean, high-contrast interfaces that strip away visual noise and feel effortless rather than cozy
facade.axisTargeted: Visual warmth vs. clarity (dark/warm vs. bright/clean)
facade.label: Bright & minimal
facade.acceptImplies: Explore light backgrounds, high contrast, maximalist whitespace, and stripped-down typography to reduce cognitive load
facade.rejectImplies: The cozy dark hypothesis gains strength; users want intimacy over efficiency in their interface experience

draft.title: QuickBite – Recipe App for People Who Hate Cooking
draft.summary: A recipe app that strips away complexity and delivers only the fastest, easiest meals with minimal ingredients and steps. Perfect for busy people who want food without the fuss.
draft.html:
QuickBite
Meals in 15 minutes or less. No excuses.
Filter by mood
Today's picks
Pasta aglio e olio
⏱ 8 min
🛒 4 items
Avocado toast
⏱ 3 min
🛒 3 items
Egg fried rice
⏱ 10 min
🛒 5 items
